msgs.text:
Turn 30 — the A2 -ghost pair splits; the real coefficient target is a smaller secondary quotient I re-read only the three files attached to this message. The problem remains the over-G isomorphism question for the quotients ΓS,ΓH defined from F=P⋊G and the relators rS,rH. The statement asks whether there is a continuous isomorphism Φ:ΓS→ΓH with πH∘Φ=πS.The current gap is exactly the one left by Turns 23–29: the residual Ef,mod2,s=1 obstruction tower is closed, but finite coefficient lifting over the local Ef-coefficient ring is not yet proved. Turn 18 established the residual induction Cm=Lm for all m≥5, so every residual even square quotient is hit by a root transgression and every odd layer is an ordinary boundary.

Turn 21 then repaired the global compactness framework by replacing degree-only quotients with coefficient-and-degree truncations P/PN,J.But Turns 23–25 correctly rejected square-zero smoothness and bare group-likeness as insufficient for coefficient lifting.

The new result of this turn is:the first A2=R/(4,t) ghost-pair complex splits off the lower 2-ghost square-neutrally.So the coefficient problem is sharper than Turn 29 left it. The full ghost-pair map is not surjective, but its failure is concentrated in a smaller secondary square quotient. The lower 2-ghost itself is harmless at the associated-graded level.Let

R:=EfΛ≃Z2 [[t]],t=s−1.

After the Turn-1 H1 -change, the dangerous Ef-row is(s2+1, s−1)=(2+2t+t2, t).ThusA2=R/(4,t):(s2+1,s−1)=(2,0),so the first 2-ghost sees the Y0 -coordinate, whileAt=R/(2,t2):(s2+1,s−1)=(0,t),so the first t-ghost is derivational rather than Frobenius-square. This was the structural split identified in Turns 24–26.

Work in root degree d, with L∙ the free restricted Lie algebra on A,B. An associated-graded degree-d correction is a triple(u0,u1,uq)∈Ld3,corresponding to lower corrections on Y0,Y1,q. The residual lower-tail operator isβd(u0,u1,uq)=[B,u0]+[A,u1]+[B,uq]∈Ld+1.

The A2 -lower ghost isgd(2)(u0,u1,uq)=u0.The square-shadow in the restricted-square quotient is, modulo ordinary degree-(2d−1) boundaries,θd(u0,u1,uq)=u0[2]+u1[2]+uq[2]∈Ld[2].

Now the important uniform observation:

U⟼(U,0,U)is a section of the lower 2-ghost on kerβd, becauseβd (U,0,U)=[B,U]+[B,U]=0 in characteristic 2, whileθd (U,0,U)=U[2]+U[2]=0.

So every lower 2-ghost U∈Ld can be killed while contributing no restricted-square shadow.

Equivalently, the associated ghost-pair image splits as im(kerβd→Ld⊕Ld[2])=Ld⊕imθd0,whereθd0:ker(Ld⊕Ld(u,v)↦[A,u]+[B,v]Ld+1)→Ld[2],θd0 (u,v)=u[2]+v[2].This refines the Turn-25 defect formula. If one kills only the lower 2-ghost, one is tempted to write a residual defect Ω+U[2]. But once the correction is required simultaneously to preserve the residual lower tail, the diagonal section (U,0,U) cancels that U[2]-contribution. The lower ghost is not the source of the obstruction; the only possible A2 -coefficient obstruction lies in the secondary quotientHd(2):=Ld[2]/imθd0.This also explains Turn 29’s rank failure structurally. The zero-ghost square-shadow map was not “too small by accident”; it is exactly the whole secondary obstruction target after the lower 2-ghost is split off.

The ranks are:ddimLd rankθd0dimHd(2)465156336114771861283615219561343101052481 So the corrected coefficient target is emphatically not surjectivity onto all of Ld[2]. The target is the actual defect class in Hd(2). If the actual A2 -coefficient defects vanish in these Hd(2), the 2-coefficient layer clears; if some defect survives, that is the first genuine finite-coefficient obstruction candidate.

In degree d=4, using the Turn-27 restricted basise0e1e2e3e4e5=AAAB+AABA+ABAA+BAAA,=AABB+BBAA,=ABBB+BABB+BBAB+BBBA,=(AB+BA)2,=A4,=B4,the one-dimensional cokernel H4(2)may be detected byμ4=coeffe1[2]+coeffe3[2].This is not the same functional as the old residual degree-16 λ-functional. Turn 27 showed that q↦qY4 has zero 2-ghost and toggles the old missing λ-direction via the B8=e5[2]shadow.

Turn 30 shows that the remaining A2 -coefficient question at d=4 is instead the μ4 -projection. So the previous qY4 seed was essential, but it did not by itself prove coefficient nonemptiness.This is a useful structural correction. The next test should not ask whetherkergd(2)→Ld[2]is surjective; Turn 29 already showed that is false. Nor should it ask whether the full pair map hits every pair (U,Ω). The lower ghost U splits off square-neutrally. The true question is:

Does the actual A2 -coefficient defect vanish in Hd(2)?

For d=4, this is one scalar, μ4. For d=5,6,7,8, the obstruction spaces have dimensions 3,7,12,21. Those are the finite coefficient classes that now matter.The t-direction remains structurally different. Since over At=R/(2,t2) the row is (0,t), its first ghost is derivational, not Frobenius-square. The same diagonal square-neutral phenomenon is specific to the 2-Witt ghost. The t-case should be tested by its derivation defect complex, but it should not be merged with the A2 square-shadow quotient.

So Turn 30 does not prove ΓS≅GΓH, and it does not produce a disproof. It replaces the too-large Turn-29 target with the correct secondary coefficient obstruction groups Hd(2). That is a uniform reduction: lower 2-ghosts are harmless in every root degree; only the secondary zero-ghost square quotient can obstruct.

ESTABLISHED: In the A2=R/(4,t) coefficient problem, the lower 2-ghost map on kerβd has the square-neutral section U↦(U,0,U). Hence the true 2-coefficient obstruction is not a full ghost-pair cokernel but the secondary quotientHd(2)=Ld[2]/imθd0.For d=4,…,10, its dimensions are 1,3,7,12,21,43,81, and the d=4 functional is μ4=coeffe1[2]+coeffe3[2].

GAP: The actual A2 -coefficient defect has not yet been projected to Hd(2). In particular, the first decisive scalar is μ4. Vanishing of the actual defects in all Hd(2), together with the separate t-derivation check, is still needed for finite coefficient nonemptiness.

NEXT: Compute the actual A2 -defect projection to H4(2), i.e. the scalar μ4. If it vanishes, continue to the H5(2),H6(2),… projections; if it is nonzero, R/(4,t) supplies the first serious finite-coefficient obstruction candidate.[TURN30-LOWER-2-GHOST-SPLITS-SECONDARY-QUOTIENT]
